name: Old Hawthorn
voicePatterns: rambling and folksy — drops significant information casually
conspiratorial asides — 'Now here's a funny thing...'
never directly answers questions about how he knows things
refers to his mule Stitch as a conversational partner
dialogueHooks: sharing rumors from towns weeks of travel away
selling useful but eclectic goods from his cart
casually mentioning details he shouldn't know
offering half-accurate maps and fully interesting stories

always: trade routes and which villages are healthy vs struggling
current prices and availability of goods across the region
rumors from Loomhearth and other settlements
which roads are safe and which have gone thin
sometimes: Wardweaver Guilds are buying double the usual thread supplies
specific villages where the wards failed recently
Threadwalker patrol routes and their gaps
rarely: his 30-year trade route data constitutes the most comprehensive record of the Thinning's advance
he's Fray-touched — temporal perception variant — and has been for 30 years
he can sense Fray activity over moderate distances and always knows the exact time

morning.activity: setting up his cart, chatting with early risers

afternoon.activity: trading goods, sharing news from other villages

evening.activity: telling stories over ale, gathering local gossip

night.activity: sleeping by his cart with Stitch, watching the stars

backstory: Hawthorn has traveled the roads of Threadhallow for decades. Thirty years ago he had a bad experience in a thin place and came out changed — he always knows the exact time, senses Fray activity at a distance, and heals slightly faster than normal. He's never told anyone. He doesn't identify with the Hollow Court. He considers himself a peddler who got some perks out of a bad situation. His real value is something even he doesn't fully appreciate: his decades of trade route data constitute the most comprehensive longitudinal record of the Thinning's advance in existence.
secret: Hawthorn is Fray-touched with the temporal perception variant. He can sense Fray activity over moderate distances and always knows the exact time. He's unknowingly mapped the Thinning's advance for 30 years through his trade routes. The Rememberers would kill for his testimony.
motivation: Travel, trade, and satisfy an endless curiosity about the world's changes
tensions: simple peddler who possesses invaluable strategic intelligence
Fray-touched but refuses to identify with the Hollow Court
knows the world is getting worse but keeps traveling anyway

knownFacts: Bryn tolerates him for his goods
bryn_oakheart.history: A business relationship with mutual wariness. Hawthorn finds her suspicion amusing.

knownFacts: Aldric is the social hub of Thornwick
aldric_tapper.history: Two information brokers who enjoy each other's company and swap rumors.
